label: Hospitals Damaged
note: Iran: 77+ healthcare facilities damaged (31 major clinical, 12 inactive) — 20+ verified healthcare attacks (WHO) — 307 health/medical facilities damaged — ~20,000 civilian buildings affected — Lebanon: 49 primary healthcare centers + 5 hospitals shut — 3.2M displaced Iran + 4M region — 115M need aid across Eastern Med
source: Iranian Red Crescent / Iranian Health Ministry / WHO / OCHA / Lebanese MoH / ICRC
detail: 307 health/medical facilities damaged per Iranian Red Crescent. 77 healthcare facilities hit. 20+ verified attacks on healthcare in Iran (WHO). ~20,000 civilian buildings affected. 65+ schools destroyed. 120+ historical/cultural heritage sites damaged including UNESCO-listed Golestan Palace. Iran: 3.2 million internally displaced (incl. ~864,000 children); 2,076+ killed (Health Min) / 3,519+ (HRANA) / 7,300+ (Hengaw); 26,500+ wounded. Lebanon: 1,345+ killed, 4,040+ wounded, ~1.2 million displaced (1/6 of population), 189,000+ crossed into Syria, 370,000+ children affected. 49 primary healthcare centers and 5 hospitals shut following Israeli evacuation orders. WHO $30.3M flash appeal (Mar-Aug). 115 million people across Eastern Med require humanitarian aid — nearly half of all people in need globally. Source: Iranian Red Crescent / OCHA / WHO / ICRC / UNHCR / HRANA.

label: Schools Destroyed
note: 65+ schools destroyed per OCHA. 30+ universities struck including Shahid Beheshti University (Apr 3). Minab school (165-180 killed). 120+ historical/cultural heritage sites damaged. Iran University of Science and Technology severely damaged. IRGC threatened retaliatory strikes on US/Israeli campuses in Qatar and UAE.
source: Iranian Red Crescent / AP / NYT / Amnesty
detail: Girls' school struck by US Tomahawk cruise missile adjacent to IRGC naval compound. Amnesty International verified 168 killed (100+ schoolchildren aged 7-12) at Shajareh Tayyebeh girls' elementary school on February 28. US internal investigation (Mar 11) determined the strike was likely due to outdated DIA intelligence — the school was once part of an IRGC compound but had been walled off since 2016. Weapons experts confirmed Tomahawk missile fragments. UN investigation initiated March 17, ongoing. HRW and Amnesty demanded accountability for potential war crimes.

label: Power Plants Hit
detail: Grid disruptions across western Iran. Isfahan, Kermanshah, Ahvaz power infrastructure degraded. Civilian blackouts reported in multiple provinces.

label: Oil/Gas Facilities Hit
note: IEA: 44+ energy assets severely damaged across 9 countries — worst since combined 1973 + 1979 oil shocks
source: IEA / DoD / Bloomberg / QatarEnergy / Reuters / satellite imagery
detail: IEA (Mar 23): At least 44 energy assets severely damaged across 9 countries — 'very severe, worse than the combined 1973 and 1979 oil shocks.' IEA released record 400M barrels from strategic stockpiles (US: 172M from SPR). Iraq declared force majeure on all foreign-operated oilfields (production collapsed from 4.2M to ~1.2-1.4M bpd). Combined Gulf production cuts exceed 10M bpd (JPMorgan). Iran: Kharg destroyed, South Pars Phases 3/4/5/6 struck. Qatar: Ras Laffan 12.8 MTPA lost (17% of LNG exports, 3-5yr repair). SAMREF (Saudi) struck. Kuwait: Mina al-Ahmadi hit. Haifa BAZAN refinery. Bahrain Bapco under force majeure. Iran sanctions lifted on ~140M barrels at sea (30 days). Jones Act waived 60 days.

label: Nuclear Facilities Struck
note: Fordow, Isfahan UCF, Natanz (3x), Bushehr (4x — auxiliary building struck Apr 4 — FM Araghchi warns radioactive fallout risk to GCC capitals — Russia evacuated 198 more staff), Arak Heavy Water, Ardakan yellowcake, Yazd — IAEA severed since Feb 26 — 408-441 kg uranium at 60% — ISIS: all ~22,000 centrifuges destroyed or inoperable — Fordow 'no longer operational' per IAEA — leaked DIA assessment questions damage claims
detail: Fordow enrichment facility struck Day 1 with B-2/GBU-57 MOPs — assessed inoperable after 12+ GBU-57 MOP strikes. Isfahan UCF struck Day 1. Natanz struck 4 times (June 2025 and repeatedly in 2026) — underground FEP 'severely damaged.' Day 29: US-Israeli airstrikes hit Ardakan yellowcake facility and Khondab Heavy Water Complex. Bushehr struck 4 times during Epic Fury — FM Araghchi warned Apr 4 repeated strikes risk radioactive fallout reaching GCC capitals. Plant holds 72 tons of fuel and 210 tons of spent fuel. IAEA denied access ~10 months. Iran possesses ~440-540 kg of uranium at 60% — theoretically enough for 10+ warheads per IAEA DG Grossi. Bulletin of Atomic Scientists: up to 540 kg transferred to underground tunnel complex at Isfahan. Fourth undeclared enrichment facility at Isfahan uninspected — IAEA doesn't know if centrifuges installed. ISIS: Israel/US attacks 'effectively destroyed Iran's centrifuge enrichment program' but residual uranium in tunnels is critical concern. Trump briefed on ground-force insertion plan for Isfahan tunnels. OFAC General License U: ~140M barrels through Apr 19.

label: Airports Damaged
detail: Mehrabad (Tehran), Shiraz TAB 6, Tabriz TAB 2 runways cratered in first wave. Dubai International Airport struck by drone Mar 16. Regional airspace closures affect 24,000+ commercial flights.

label: Bridges Destroyed
note: Western Iran + all Litani River bridges in Lebanon destroyed
detail: Iran: Road and rail bridges in Kermanshah, Lorestan, and Khuzestan provinces destroyed to degrade IRGC logistics. Lebanon: IDF claims all bridges over Litani destroyed. Israel declares 'security zone' south of Litani River (~30 km) — DM Katz says displaced Lebanese 'will not return south of the Litani.' Defense Minister Katz ordered demolition of border villages using 'Beit Hanoun and Rafah models.'
